_meta.tower_name: Tower of Prime Wiki + Community Browsing
_meta.dna: wiki(community) = extract(page) x compress(pzip) x push(firestore) x pull(cache) x render(mermaid) x search(index) x codebook(offline)
_meta.question_expansion_instruction: Each floor has 7 seed questions from a specific persona context. Questions must be answerable by checking if something actually works — click it, see it, verify it. Not theoretical.

name: Tower of Prime Wiki + Community Browsing
protocol: FORECAST -> CROSS-REF -> AUDIT(7q) -> FIX -> VERIFY -> SEAL -> POSTMORTEM

floors.persona_context: 29, Bangalore, Technical writer, Expert tech, Pro tier, wants to extract documentation pages from MDN and DevDocs into Prime Wiki
questions: Can I navigate to a URL in Solace Browser and click 'Extract to Prime Wiki' to start the extraction process?
Does the extraction capture the page's headings, body text, code blocks, and metadata (title, description, author) correctly?
Can I see a preview of the extracted content before it is pushed to the community wiki?
Does the extractor use CPU-structural mode (regex-based) by default so it works without an LLM API key?
If the page has dynamic content loaded via JavaScript, does the extraction wait for the page to fully render before capturing?
Can I extract a multi-page documentation section (e.g., 5 pages of MDN CSS reference) in a single batch operation?
Does the extraction preserve code syntax highlighting information so code blocks render correctly in the wiki?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Maya extracts technical documentation. Extraction must handle real-world pages with JS rendering, code blocks, and multi-page flows. CPU-structural mode is the default path.

floors.persona_context: 41, Copenhagen, Data engineer, Expert tech, Pro tier, interested in compression ratios and storage costs
questions: After extraction, does the app show the original page size and the compressed size with the compression ratio (e.g., 2.7:1 gzip)?
Can I see the compression method used (gzip currently, PZip when available) in the extraction result details?
Does the compressed snapshot include all extracted fields (headings, body, meta, CTAs) without losing any content?
Can I compare the decompressed snapshot against the original page to verify no data was lost during compression?
Does the app show the estimated storage cost per snapshot (e.g., $0.0001 per Firestore write) before pushing?
If the page is very large (>1MB), does the compression still complete within 5 seconds on a standard machine?
Can I see cumulative storage stats showing total snapshots stored, total compressed size, and average compression ratio?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Viktor cares about data integrity and efficiency. Compression must be lossless, measurable, and cost-transparent. Lossy compression of technical content is unacceptable.

floors.persona_context: 35, Dakar, Open-source contributor, Intermediate tech, Starter tier, wants to share extracted knowledge with the community
questions: After extracting and compressing a page, can I click 'Push to Community' and see the snapshot uploaded to Firestore?
Does the push operation return a confirmation with the URL hash (SHA-256 of the original URL) as the document ID?
Can I see the pushed snapshot in the Prime Wiki stats endpoint (/api/v1/prime-wiki/stats) with my contribution counted?
If I try to push a duplicate URL that already exists in the wiki, does the system update the existing record or create a versioned entry?
Does the push operation require authentication so anonymous users cannot pollute the community wiki?
Can I see a list of all my contributions to the community wiki from my profile page?
If the Firestore write fails (quota, network), does the app show the specific error and offer to retry?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Aisha contributes to open knowledge. Push must be authenticated, deduplicated, and give her credit for contributions. Silent failures waste her time.

floors.persona_context: 24, Montreal, Computer science student, Beginner tech, Free tier, wants to pull community knowledge for offline study
questions: Can I search the Prime Wiki from the Solace Browser and pull a snapshot by URL hash to view it locally?
Does the pull response include the full decompressed snapshot with headings, body, and code blocks rendered correctly?
Can I pull a snapshot while offline if it was previously cached locally during an earlier session?
Does the pull operation show the snapshot's metadata (contributor, extraction date, source URL) alongside the content?
If I pull a snapshot that does not exist, does the API return a clear 'not found' message instead of an empty response?
Can I pull multiple snapshots in batch (e.g., all CSS-related snapshots) for offline study?
Does the pulled content render in a readable format with proper heading hierarchy and code block formatting?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Lucas is a student on Free tier. Pull must work offline (cached), handle missing records gracefully, and render content readably. Complex API workflows are a barrier.

floors.persona_context: 37, Moscow, Solutions architect, Expert tech, Pro tier, uses Mermaid diagrams extensively and wants them rendered in wiki snapshots
questions: If the extracted page contains Mermaid diagram code blocks, does the wiki snapshot preserve the raw Mermaid syntax?
When viewing a pulled snapshot that contains Mermaid diagrams, does the viewer render them as visual diagrams (not raw text)?
Can I click on a rendered Mermaid diagram to see the source code and copy it for use in my own documentation?
Does the Mermaid renderer support flowcharts, sequence diagrams, class diagrams, and state diagrams?
If a Mermaid code block has a syntax error, does the renderer show an error message pointing to the broken line?
Can I export a wiki snapshot with rendered Mermaid diagrams as a PDF that includes the visual diagrams?
Does the Mermaid rendering work offline using a local renderer (not a cloud API call)?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Elena depends on Mermaid diagrams for architecture documentation. Rendering must be local, support major diagram types, and handle errors gracefully.

floors.persona_context: 42, Taipei, DevRel lead at API company, Expert tech, Pro tier, wants to search the community wiki for relevant content to share
questions: Can I search the Prime Wiki using the /api/v1/prime-wiki/search?q=react endpoint and get results matching my query?
Does the search return results with title, snippet, source URL, contributor, and relevance score?
Can I filter search results by content type (documentation, tutorial, reference, blog post)?
Does the search endpoint support partial matching so searching 'react hook' finds 'React Custom Hooks Tutorial'?
Can I search from the Solace Browser UI (not just the API) with a search bar on the Prime Wiki page?
Does the search return results within 2 seconds for a wiki with 1000+ snapshots?
If the search returns no results, does it suggest alternative queries or related topics?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Trevor searches on behalf of his developer community. Search must be fast, relevant, and support both API and UI access. Empty results without suggestions are dead ends.

floors.persona_context: 31, Helsinki, Software consultant who travels to client sites with limited internet, Expert tech, Pro tier, needs offline access to technical references
questions: Can I select multiple wiki snapshots and click 'Create Codebook' to bundle them into an offline-accessible collection?
Does the codebook include a table of contents generated from the snapshot titles and headings?
Can I open and search the codebook when my laptop is completely offline (airplane mode)?
Does the codebook include all code blocks, Mermaid diagrams, and formatting from the original snapshots?
Can I update a codebook by syncing new snapshots when I am back online without recreating the entire collection?
Does the codebook show the last sync date so I know if my offline reference is stale?
Can I export the codebook as a single HTML file that I can share with a colleague who does not have Solace installed?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Sarah works offline at client sites. Codebooks must be truly offline (not just cached), searchable, updatable, and shareable. 'Requires internet' is a FAIL for every question.
